info.name: KikooAI Backend
info.description:
Complete API collection for the KikooAI English learning + interview prep platform backend.

**Setup:**
1. Set `baseUrl` variable to `http://localhost:3000`
2. Run **Signup** or **Login** — tokens are auto-saved.
3. All authenticated routes use `{{accessToken}}` automatically.

**Audio Flow:**
`POST /audio/upload-init` → upload directly to Cloudinary → `POST /audio/complete` → poll `GET /jobs/:id/status` → `GET /audio/:id/transcript`

request.description: Checks server status and database connectivity. No auth required.

Register a new user. Role must be one of: `student`, `job_seeker`, `professional`.

Auto-saves `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables.

response.name: 409 Duplicate user
400 Validation Error

request.description:
Authenticate an existing user. Auto-saves `accessToken` and `refreshToken`.

**Seeded test users** (run `npm run seed:users` first):
- `jobseeker@kikoo.test` / `JobSeeker1234!` — resume & interview flows
- `student@kikoo.test` / `Student1234!`
- `admin@kikoo.test` / `Admin1234!` — admin routes

exec: const label = json.data.isNewUser ? 'Google signup successful' : 'Google login successful';

request.description:
Sign in or sign up via Google OAuth. The mobile app obtains an `idToken` from Google Sign-In SDK and sends it here.

**`role` and `username`** are required only for brand-new users. For returning users they are ignored.

Auto-saves `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables.

**`isNewUser`** in the response tells the app whether to show an onboarding screen.

response.name: 422 Missing signup fields

request.description: Rotate the refresh token. Old token is invalidated, new pair is issued.
Invalidates all refresh tokens for the authenticated user.
Returns the authenticated user joined with their full profile (XP, streak, badges, subscription, etc.).
Update profile fields. All fields optional.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Fetch a random exercise seed. `answer_key` and `acceptable_variants` are **stripped** before returning.

**Valid types:** `fillup`, `jumbled_word`, `jumbled_sentence`, `vocab`, `synonyms`, `antonyms`, `pronunciation_spelling`, `grammar_transform`, `typing_from_audio`

response.name: 200 OK
404 No exercises found
200 OK

request.description:
Submit an answer for any exercise type.

- `user_answer`: string for text types, option ID (`"a"`,`"b"`,`"c"`,`"d"`) for vocab/synonyms/antonyms
- Consumes 1 energy, awards XP, updates streak if threshold met.

response.name: 200 Correct
200 Wrong
402 Energy Depleted

request.description:
Returns a speaking prompt matched to the user's current XP level.

| XP | CEFR | Difficulty |
|---|---|---|
| 0–300 | A1/A2 | easy |
| 301–1500 | B1/B2 | medium |
| 1501+ | C1 | hard |

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Evaluate a speaking exercise submission.

- `audio_id`: UUID from a completed audio transcription
- `prompt_id`: UUID of the speaking prompt seed

Reuses existing `feedback_json` if already analysed. Composite score = average of pronunciation + vocabulary + grammar + fluency. Awards XP and updates streak.

response.name: 200 OK — Evaluated
202 Transcript Still Processing

request.description:
**Step 1 of the audio flow.**

Generates a signed Cloudinary upload URL. The client should use the returned params to upload the audio file **directly to Cloudinary** — it never passes through the backend server.

**context_type** values: `speaking` | `interview` | `speed_reading`

**format** values: `webm` | `mp4` | `mp3` | `wav` | `ogg` | `m4a`

The signature is valid for 15 minutes (`expires_in_seconds: 900`).

**Step 2 — done by the client, NOT through this backend.**

Upload the audio file directly to Cloudinary using the params from Step 1.

Cloudinary will return a JSON response containing `public_id` and `secure_url` — save these for Step 3.

Example Cloudinary response:
```json
{
  "public_id": "kikoo/audio/user-id/abc123",
  "secure_url": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dv0xgum7s/video/upload/v1234/kikoo/audio/abc123.webm",
  "duration": 45.2,
  "format": "webm"
}
```

**Step 3 of the audio flow.**

Tell the backend about the completed Cloudinary upload. The backend will:
1. Verify the asset exists on Cloudinary
2. Update the `audio_files` record
3. Enqueue a transcription job (Gemini)

Auto-saves `audioId` and `jobId` to collection variables.

response.name: 404 Audio record not found / wrong owner

request.description:
**Step 4 — poll every 2–3 seconds until `status` is `done` or `failed`.**

`data.type` identifies the job: `transcription`, `resume_analyze`, `resume_roast`, `interview_score`.

Progress stages:
- `10%` — Downloading audio from Cloudinary
- `30%` — Audio downloaded
- `60%` — Transcription complete
- `80%` — Feedback analysis complete
- `100%` — Done, transcript saved

Jobs are user-scoped — returns 404 for jobs that belong to a different user.

response.name: 200 Failed

request.description:
**Step 5 — fetch after job status is `done`.**

Returns the full transcript and structured AI feedback.

If transcription is not yet complete, returns `202` with the current status.

response.name: 200 Done — Full transcript

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's 20 most recent audio files, ordered by creation date descending.

Permanently deletes the audio file from Cloudinary and all associated records from the database (audio_files + transcripts cascade).

Used for GDPR deletion requests.

item.name: Resumes
item.description:
Resume upload (file or JSON), AI analysis, roast, and management.

**Quick test (JSON resume):**
1. **Auth → Login** (saves `accessToken`)
2. **Save JSON Resume** (saves `resumeId`)
3. **Analyze Resume** — expect **202** with `data.job_id`, `data.status: "pending"`, `data.report_id` (not your request body echoed)
4. **Get Report (poll)** or **Jobs → Get Job Status** every 2–3s until `done` or `failed`

**File upload flow:**
`POST /resumes/upload-init` → upload to Cloudinary raw endpoint → `POST /resumes/upload-complete` → `POST /resumes/analyze` → poll `/resumes/reports/:report_id`

**JSON flow:**
`POST /resumes/save-json` → `POST /resumes/analyze` or `/roast`

**File view:**
`GET /resumes/:id/file` — 10-minute signed URL (file resumes only).

request.description:
**Step 1 of the resume file upload flow.**

Generates a signed Cloudinary upload URL for raw files (PDF/DOCX/TXT). No body required.

Creates a placeholder `resumes` row and returns its `resume_id` so you can reference it in /upload-complete.

The upload URL targets Cloudinary's `/raw/upload` endpoint.

**Step 2 — Upload directly to Cloudinary.**

This request goes to Cloudinary, NOT the backend. Use the params returned from upload-init.

Save `public_id` and `secure_url` from the response for the next step.

**Step 3 — Confirm the Cloudinary upload.**

Verifies the asset exists on Cloudinary and updates the resume record.

`format`: `pdf` | `docx` | `txt`
`title` is optional (max 100 chars).

response.name: 400 File not on Cloudinary

request.description:
Save structured resume data as JSON — no file upload needed.

Auto-saves `resumeId` to collection variables.

Trigger an ATS-style AI analysis of the resume against a job description.

`jd_text` must be at least 50 characters.
`cover_letter` is optional.

Returns **202** with `{ success, data: { job_id, status: "pending", report_id, message } }` — read **`json.data`**, not the request body.

Auto-saves `reportId` and `jobId`. Test script asserts `status === "pending"` and that the response is not an echo of the request.

Poll **Get Report (poll)** or **Jobs → Get Job Status** every 2–3s until `done` or `failed`.

response.name: 400 JD too short

request.description:
Same as analyze but returns witty roast comments in `roast_lines`.

Returns **202** with `data.status: "pending"`, `data.job_id`, `data.report_id`. Auto-saves `reportId` and `jobId`.

Poll for analysis results.

- **202** — still processing (`data.status` pending/processing)
- **200** + `data.status === "done"` — full report in `data.report`
- **200** + `data.status === "failed"` — business failure: `code` **ANALYSIS_FAILED**, plus `failure_code` (e.g. **GEMINI_QUOTA_EXCEEDED**), `failure_detail`, `job_id`

response.name: 200 Done — analyze
200 Done — roast
200 Failed — Gemini quota

request.description: Returns up to 20 most recent resumes for the authenticated user (newest first).

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Returns a short-lived (10-minute) signed Cloudinary URL so the client can view or download the resume file.

Only valid for file-based resumes (PDF/DOCX/TXT). Returns 400 RESUME_NO_FILE for JSON-only resumes.

Do **not** cache the URL — fetch a fresh one each time the user wants to view the file.
Permanently deletes the resume and all associated reports. If a file resume, also removes from Cloudinary.

item.name: Interview
item.description:
Mock interview rooms with AI-scored per-question feedback.

**Live WebRTC flow:**
`POST /interview/live/start` → Flutter joins returned LiveKit room → AI agent bridges LiveKit audio to Gemini Live → `POST /interview/live/:room_id/end` → poll `/interview/live/:room_id/status` or `GET /rooms/:id/result`.

**Recorded flow:**
`POST /interview/rooms/create` → record audio via audio pipeline → `POST /rooms/:id/record/start` → `POST /rooms/:id/record/stop` with audio_id → poll `/jobs/:job_id/status` → `GET /rooms/:id/result`

**Quick evaluate (no room):**
`POST /interview/questions/evaluate` with question_text + answer_text

request.description: Returns non-secret live interview metadata: LiveKit transport URL, live/analysis model IDs, and voice map (emma/john). Gemini credentials stay server-side; use POST /interview/live/start to receive a scoped LiveKit WebRTC token.
Creates a LiveKit-backed live interview room and returns a short-lived WebRTC token. The Flutter app joins the returned LiveKit room; Gemini credentials remain on the backend/agent side.
Marks the LiveKit interview ended. The media worker should finalize transcript/report through the internal agent completion callback.
Returns live session lifecycle and agent status for the Flutter UI.

Generates 12 role-specific interview questions via Gemini. Results cached in-memory for 1 hour per role+round+difficulty combo to avoid redundant API calls.

Params:
- role (required)
- round (default: Technical)
- difficulty (default: Medium)

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Called by the client after the Gemini Live session ends and the analysis model has generated the report. Persists transcript + report to interview_rooms.result_json, sets status = done.

Use GET /interview/rooms/:id/result to retrieve the saved report later.

Create a new interview room. All fields optional.

- `difficulty`: `easy` | `medium` | `hard`
- `questions`: max 20 items

Auto-saves `roomId` to collection variables.

response.name: 201 Created

request.description:
Mark the interview room as recording. No body required.

Call this when the user clicks Start — use this timestamp for the UI timer.

response.name: 400 Already active

request.description:
Stop recording and trigger AI processing.

Requires `audio_id` from the audio pipeline (`POST /audio/complete`).

Auto-saves `jobId`. Poll `/jobs/:job_id/status` then fetch result.

Fetch the interview result. Returns `result: null` if not done yet.

Poll `/jobs/:job_id/status` first and only call this when status = done.

response.name: 200 Done

request.description: Returns up to 20 most recent interview rooms for the authenticated user.

Evaluate any question + answer immediately — no room needed.

At least one of `answer_text` or `audio_id` required.

Great for browser extensions or quick practice.

response.name: 200 OK
400 No answer provided

request.description:
Returns the user's personalized daily exercise set based on their XP/CEFR level.

XP to difficulty:
- XP <= 300  → easy  (A1/A2)
- XP <= 1500 → medium (B1/B2)
- XP >  1500 → hard   (C1/C2)

Returns 6 exercises (fillup, jumbled_sentence, vocab, synonyms, grammar_transform, speaking_prompt). answer_key is stripped. Falls back to medium/easy difficulty if no seed exists at the user's level.

progress.completed_today counts all exercise_submissions made today by this user.

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: Jobs
item.description:
Background job management. All AI tasks (transcription, resume analysis, interview scoring) run as background jobs.

Use `GET /jobs/:job_id/status` to poll a specific job (response includes **`type`**: `transcription` | `resume_analyze` | `resume_roast` | `interview_score`, plus `error_code` when failed). Use `GET /jobs` to list all recent jobs.

request.description:
Returns the 20 most recent jobs for the authenticated user (newest first).

Includes job types: `transcription`, `resume_analyze`, `resume_roast`, `interview_score`.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "jobs": [
      {
        "id": "uuid",
        "type": "resume_analyze",
        "status": "done",
        "progress_pct": 100,
        "error_message": null,
        "error_code": null,
        "created_at": "2026-03-29T18:00:00.000Z",
        "updated_at": "2026-03-29T18:00:30.000Z"
      },
      {
        "id": "uuid",
        "type": "resume_roast",
        "status": "done",
        "progress_pct": 100,
        "error_message": null,
        "error_code": null,
        "created_at": "2026-03-29T17:58:00.000Z",
        "updated_at": "2026-03-29T17:58:25.000Z"
      },
      {
        "id": "uuid",
        "type": "transcription",
        "status": "done",
        "progress_pct": 100,
        "error_message": null,
        "error_code": null,
        "created_at": "2026-03-29T17:55:00.000Z",
        "updated_at": "2026-03-29T17:55:20.000Z"
      }
    ]
  }
}

request.description:
Poll background job progress. Uses `{{jobId}}` from **Analyze Resume** or **Roast Resume**.

For resume analysis: `data.type` is `resume_analyze` or `resume_roast`. Poll every 2–3s until `status` is `done` or `failed`.

Resume progress: ~10% start → ~30% text loaded → ~80% AI analysis → 100% done.

On failure, check `error_code` / `error_message`, then **Get Report (poll)** for `ANALYSIS_FAILED` details.

response.name: 200 Failed — Gemini quota

item.name: Learning Path
item.description: 30-day learning path progress sync. Progress resets automatically every 30 days. All endpoints require a valid JWT.

request.description:
Returns the authenticated user's 30-day learning path status.

- Auto-creates a new cycle on first call.
- Resets cycle (and clears completed tasks) if 30 days have elapsed since `cycle_start`.
- `current_day` is 1-indexed (day 1 = cycle start day).
- `days_remaining` = 30 − current_day.
- `completed_tasks` lists all task UUIDs completed in the current cycle.

Marks a task as completed for the authenticated user.

- **Idempotent** — re-submitting the same `task_id` is safe and returns 200 with no duplicate DB row.
- `task_id`: UUID of the frontend task.
- `day`: integer 1–30 (the day in the cycle this task belongs to).

response.name: 400 Validation Error

request.description:
Get a random active game seed for the given type. Valid types: `conexo`, `speed_reading`, `contextooo`, `word_blitz`, `guess_the_word`.

`answer_key` is stripped before returning — use `POST /games/contextooo/rank` to check guesses.

response.name: 200 OK
404 No Games Available

request.description:
Rank a word guess against the secret word using Gemini semantic embeddings (gemini-embedding-001).

- `seedId`: the game `id` from `GET /games/contextooo/seed`
- `guess`: the word to rank
- `rank 1` = exact semantic match to the secret word
- `rank` ranges from 1 (closest) to 1000 (furthest)
- `similarity` is the raw cosine similarity score (0–1)

response.name: 400 Validation Error

request.description:
Submit a final score for any game type. Returns the player's leaderboard rank among all submissions for that game.

- `game_id`: UUID of the game (from seed response)
- `score`: integer score
- `combo`, `hearts_left`, `time_taken_seconds`: optional metadata

response.name: 400 Validation Error
